EN-ES Printable Resource

Bilingual Calendar & Time Labels (K-2)

A comprehensive set of bilingual (English-Spanish) labels for K-2 classrooms, focusing on days of the week, months of the year, and key calendar vocabulary. This pack supports daily routines and language acquisition.

TopicDays & Months
Age BandK-2
Pack Typeclassroom labels
LanguageEN-ES

✨ What's Inside

  • 20 classroom labels
  • 5 routine cards
  • 4 classroom rules
  • Teacher notes & answer key

📖 How to Use

Print and laminate full-size labels for a prominent daily calendar display (e.g., 'Today is...', 'The Month is...').Use small-size month labels on a rotating 'Month of the Year' wheel or a linear display.Integrate day labels into a daily calendar routine where students physically change the day.Attach visual routine cards to a daily schedule board, pointing to each as the routine progresses.Display classroom rules prominently, reviewing them daily or as needed.

Classroom Rules

1

Listen when others speak.

Escucha cuando otros hablan.

2

Raise your hand to speak.

Levanta la mano para hablar.

3

Be kind and respectful.

Sé amable y respetuoso.

4

Walk, don't run.

Camina, no corras.

Mar 18, 2026lanternell.comPage 5 / 6
LanternELLTeacher Notes📄 Printable Pack

Teacher Notes

🎯 Objective

To provide a highly visual and interactive environment for K-2 students to learn and practice days of the week, months of the year, and key calendar phrases in both English and Spanish, fostering early biliteracy and routine comprehension.

💡 Suggested Use

Print and laminate full-size labels for a prominent daily calendar display (e.g., 'Today is...', 'The Month is...').Use small-size month labels on a rotating 'Month of the Year' wheel or a linear display.Integrate day labels into a daily calendar routine where students physically change the day.Attach visual routine cards to a daily schedule board, pointing to each as the routine progresses.Display classroom rules prominently, reviewing them daily or as needed.

📦 Materials Needed

  • Printer
  • Laminator (optional, but recommended for durability)
  • Velcro dots or tape
  • Wall space or bulletin board
  • Pointer stick (for calendar time)

🔀 Differentiation Tips

  • For beginners: Focus on repeating the target vocabulary daily. Use gestures and TPR (Total Physical Response).
  • For developing learners: Encourage sentence frames (e.g., 'Today is [day].', 'El mes es [month].'). Ask simple questions.
  • For advanced learners: Have them lead the calendar routine, identify patterns (e.g., days that start with 'M'), or create simple sentences about the day/month.
📄 Terms of Use — personal-classroom-use
✅ Print for your students & classroom use.❌ Do not share, redistribute, or resell files.🏫 School license: support@lanternell.com
